subtopic: India's RE Status and International Solar Alliance
title: Evolution of Financing for Sustainable Development
focus: Key international agreements and frameworks for sustainable development financing including Monterrey, Doha, and Addis Ababa.
rationale: Provides a distinct historical timeline of UN financing frameworks, which is highly testable for international environmental and development conventions.
sectionExcerpt: Monterrey Consensus (2002) - Marked beginning of Financing for Development process at United Nations. It focused on increased Official Development Assistance (ODA)... Doha Declaration (2008) - It reaffirmed Monterrey Consensus... laid the foundation for Green Climate Fund. Addis Ababa Action Agenda (2015) - It introduced new financing frameworks for sustainable development such as Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs), Technology Bank for Least Developed Countries etc.
evidenceBullets: The Monterrey Consensus (2002) marked the beginning of the UN Financing for Development process, focusing on ODA and governance reforms.
The Doha Declaration (2008) laid the foundation for the Green Climate Fund and added gender and environment understandings to financing.
The Addis Ababa Action Agenda (2015) introduced Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs) and the Technology Bank for Least Developed Countries.

subtopic: India's RE Status and International Solar Alliance
title: India's Renewable Energy Sector Status and Targets
focus: India's RE milestones, NDC targets, Panchamrita goals, and current installed capacity statistics.
rationale: Core UPSC syllabus topic containing critical data points such as achieving 50% non-fossil capacity 5 years ahead of schedule and global rankings.
sectionExcerpt: In 2025 India's renewable energy sector achieved two historic milestones - renewable energy met 51.5% of total electricity demand (July 2025) and share of installed electricity capacity of non-fossil fuel sources reached 51% (September 2025). Achieving 50% of cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030 (achieved five years ahead)... Installed electricity capacity of non-fossil fuel sources: 256.09 GW... Global Ranking: 4th in Renewable Energy Installed Capacity.
evidenceBullets: India achieved its NDC target of 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuels five years ahead of the 2030 deadline.
Panchamrita targets for 2030 include meeting 50% of energy requirements from RE and reaching 500 GW of non-fossil energy capacity.
Solar holds the largest share (39.4%) of India's non-fossil fuel installed capacity, followed by Wind (10.6%) and Hydro (10%).
India ranks 4th globally in Renewable Energy Installed Capacity and Wind Power, and 3rd in Solar Power capacity.
The top 5 renewable energy states in India are Rajasthan, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Maharashtra.

subtopic: India's RE Status and International Solar Alliance
title: International Solar Alliance (ISA) and Recent Initiatives
focus: ISA's 8th Session highlights, SUNRISE initiative, membership rules, and the 'Towards 1000' strategy.
rationale: ISA is a major India-led international organization. The 'Towards 1000' strategy, SUNRISE initiative, and universal UN member eligibility are prime targets for prelims statements.
sectionExcerpt: Mauritius Became 1st African Country to sign ISA's CPF... Launch of SUNRISE (Solar Upcycling Network for Recycling, Innovation & Stakeholder Engagement) - a global initiative on circularity and end-of-life (EoL) management of solar PV and batteries... Following 2020 amendment to its Framework Agreement, all UN members are now eligible to join. Mission: Guided by its Towards 1000 strategy, which aims to Mobilize USD 1,000 billions of investments... Deliver energy access to 1,000 million people...
evidenceBullets: Mauritius became the first African country to sign the ISA's Country Partnership Framework (CPF).
SUNRISE is a global initiative launched at the 8th ISA Assembly focusing on circularity and end-of-life management of solar PV and batteries.
Following a 2020 amendment, all UN members are eligible to join the ISA, which currently has 104 members that have signed and ratified the agreement.

ISA's 'Towards 1000' strategy aims to mobilize USD 1,000 billion in investments, deliver energy access to 1,000 million people, install 1,000 GW of solar capacity, and mitigate 1,000 million tonnes of CO2 annually by 2030.
Key ISA initiatives include the SolarX Startup Challenge, STAR-C Initiative, Global Solar Facility, and Green Hydrogen Innovation Centre.

subtopic: India's RE Status and International Solar Alliance
title: Solar Panel Efficiency Norms and ALMM
focus: MNRE's easing of efficiency norms for off-grid rural solar projects and the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM).
rationale: Policy changes regarding ALMM and Distributed Renewable Energy (DRE) are highly relevant for schemes and rural electrification topics.
sectionExcerpt: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) eased solar panel efficiency norms for off-grid rural projects allowing lower efficiency modules to be included in Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM)... New Standard: Both technologies at 18% efficiency. Scope of Change: Applies only to off-grid solar applications below 200W like Solar lanterns, Micro solar grids... Introduction of Distributed Renewable Energy (DRE) category: To support smaller manufacturers & Wider rural electrification.
evidenceBullets: MNRE eased solar panel efficiency norms to 18% for both Crystalline silicon and Cadmium Telluride technologies for off-grid rural projects below 200W.
The Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) is an MNRE-approved list of solar PV modules and manufacturers.
The relaxed efficiency norms apply only to off-grid applications like solar lanterns and micro solar grids, and do not affect larger systems like rooftop solar or pumps.
A new Distributed Renewable Energy (DRE) category was introduced to support smaller manufacturers and promote wider rural electrification.
